purpose: Safe Unreal Editor command contract between the UE5 plugin and the FastAPI backend.

transport.future_plugin_execution: Editor-native action handlers invoked only after explicit plugin-side validation

commands.name: tool_catalog
commands.description: Fetch the backend's current orchestration tools and Unreal editor capability catalog before starting a task.

commands.name: plugin_tool
commands.description: Dispatch a concrete Unreal-oriented tool request through one normalized backend route.

commands.name: plugin_chat
commands.description: Primary plugin request path for free-form questions and broader implementation goals. The backend may answer directly, return a single plan, or start a live agent session.

commands.name: selection_context
commands.description: Send current editor selection for explanation and asset-aware analysis.

commands.name: deep_asset_analysis
commands.description: Analyze pasted/exported graph or state text for the current asset.

commands.name: asset_scaffold
commands.description: Generate a safe scaffold plan for a new asset without editor mutation.

commands.name: asset_edit_plan
commands.description: Generate a controlled edit plan for a selected asset.

commands.name: agent_session
commands.description: Start or continue a staged backend workflow for multi-step goals that may require confirmation before editor-side changes.

commands.name: execute_editor_action
commands.description: Future safe editor-native action returned by the backend and executed only by the plugin after validation and user confirmation.

safety_rules: The backend may suggest editor_action objects, but the plugin is the final safety gate.
The backend may also return a live agent session with steps, pending confirmation, and next actions; the plugin should treat that as resumable workflow state rather than a one-shot answer.
The plugin can fetch /agent-tools to understand which orchestration steps and Unreal-facing tools are implemented versus still planned.
When approval should immediately continue backend-side follow-up planning, prefer /agent-session/{task_id}/confirm-and-continue over separate confirm and resume calls.
The plugin must reject any action whose target asset no longer matches the current editor state.
All mutating actions should support dry_run first.
Renames and edits should require explicit user confirmation in the plugin UI.
Binary asset mutation should happen only through Unreal editor APIs, never by direct file patching.
